req.title: Card PIN change (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall change their card PIN at most 5 times per 24-hour window. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates a 5-per-24h rate limit on card PIN changes. Test coverage requires: (1) a positive EP case confirming a single valid PIN change succeeds (HTTP 200), (2) a NEG case confirming an unauthorised caller is rejected (HTTP 401/403), (3) a BVA boundary case at exactly the 5th attempt succeeding and the 6th returning 429, and (4) an STC case walking the state machine from zero attempts through the limit transition. A DT case covers the cross-product of ownership vs. rate-limit conditions. Each test resets state via a dedicated fixture that rewinds the attempt counter, making tests idempotent.

tests.name: test_pin_change_single_valid_request_succeeds
preconditions: Card ID belongs to the authenticated customer
PIN change attempt counter is reset to 0 for this card in the last 24h
actions: PATCH /cards/{id}/pin with a valid new PIN and valid auth token
Assert response body confirms PIN updated
expected: Response JSON contains status='updated' or equivalent success indicator
trace: EP: valid partition — authenticated owner, counter < limit
tests.executable:
def test_pin_change_single_valid_request_succeeds(api_client, card_with_reset_counter):
    card_id = card_with_reset_counter["id"]
    new_pin = <generate_valid_pin>()
    response = api_client.patch(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/pin",
        json={"new_pin": new_pin},
        headers=<auth_header>(card_with_reset_counter["owner_token"]),
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "updated"

tests.name: test_pin_change_unauthenticated_caller_rejected
preconditions: No valid auth token is supplied (missing or expired)
Card ID is valid and exists in the system
actions: Assert response body contains an error message
expected: Response body contains error field indicating auth failure
trace: NEG: invalid partition — missing/invalid auth token
tests.executable:
def test_pin_change_unauthenticated_caller_rejected(api_client, existing_card_id):
    response = api_client.patch(
        f"/cards/{existing_card_id}/pin",
        json={"new_pin": <generate_valid_pin>()},
    )
    assert response.status_code in (401, 403)
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

tests.name: test_pin_change_fifth_attempt_succeeds_sixth_returns_429
preconditions: Card ID belongs to the authenticated customer
PIN change attempt counter is reset to 0 for this card in the last 24h
actions: Issue 4 successful PATCH /cards/{id}/pin requests to advance counter to 4
expected: Response on 429 includes Retry-After header or error body
trace: R-FIN-072: at most 5 times per 24-hour window
BVA: boundary values N=5 (last valid) and N+1=6 (first invalid)
tests.executable:
def test_pin_change_fifth_attempt_succeeds_sixth_returns_429(
    api_client, card_with_reset_counter
):
    card_id = card_with_reset_counter["id"]
    headers = <auth_header>(card_with_reset_counter["owner_token"])
    for i in range(4):
        r = api_client.patch(
            f"/cards/{card_id}/pin",
            json={"new_pin": <generate_valid_pin>()},
            headers=headers,
        )
        assert r.status_code == 200, f"Attempt {i+1} failed unexpectedly"
    fifth = api_client.patch(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/pin",
        json={"new_pin": <generate_valid_pin>()},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert fifth.status_code == 200
    sixth = api_client.patch(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/pin",
        json={"new_pin": <generate_valid_pin>()},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert sixth.status_code == 429
    assert "Retry-After" in sixth.headers or "error" in sixth.json()

tests.name: test_pin_change_state_transition_zero_to_limit_to_locked
preconditions: Card PIN change counter is in state ZERO (0 attempts in last 24h)
actions: Verify counter state is ZERO via <get_pin_change_count> helper
Issue 5 successful PIN change requests, verifying counter increments each time
Issue one more PIN change request
Verify response is 429 and counter state is LOCKED (>= 5)
expected: HTTP 200 for each of the first 5 transitions
trace: R-FIN-072: at most 5 times per 24-hour window
STC: covers all state transitions in the PIN change rate-limit FSM
tests.executable:
def test_pin_change_state_transition_zero_to_limit_to_locked(
    api_client, card_with_reset_counter
):
    card_id = card_with_reset_counter["id"]
    headers = <auth_header>(card_with_reset_counter["owner_token"])
    assert <get_pin_change_count>(card_id) == 0
    for expected_count in range(1, 6):
        r = api_client.patch(
            f"/cards/{card_id}/pin",
            json={"new_pin": <generate_valid_pin>()},
            headers=headers,
        )
        assert r.status_code == 200
        assert <get_pin_change_count>(card_id) == expected_count
    assert <get_pin_change_count>(card_id) == 5
    locked_r = api_client.patch(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/pin",
        json={"new_pin": <generate_valid_pin>()},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert locked_r.status_code == 429
    assert <get_pin_change_count>(card_id) >= 5

tests.name: test_pin_change_decision_table_ownership_vs_rate_limit
preconditions: Two customer tokens: owner_token (card owner) and other_token (different customer)
Card belongs only to owner_token customer
PIN change counter for owner is AT_LIMIT (5 attempts in last 24h)
actions: Case A: other_token requests PIN change on card -> expect 401 or 403 (ownership fails regardless of limit)
Case B: owner_token requests PIN change when counter == 5 -> expect 429 (ownership OK, limit exceeded)
Case C: owner_token requests PIN change when counter == 0 -> expect 200 (ownership OK, limit OK)
trace: R-FIN-072: error pathways — unauthorised and exceeded limits
DT: cross-product of {ownership valid/invalid} x {rate limit ok/exceeded}
tests.executable:
def test_pin_change_decision_table_ownership_vs_rate_limit(
    api_client, card_at_limit, fresh_card
):
    other_headers = <auth_header>(<other_customer_token>())
    owner_headers_limited = <auth_header>(card_at_limit["owner_token"])
    owner_headers_fresh = <auth_header>(fresh_card["owner_token"])
    case_a = api_client.patch(
        f"/cards/{card_at_limit['id']}/pin",
        json={"new_pin": <generate_valid_pin>()},
        headers=other_headers,
    )
    assert case_a.status_code in (401, 403), "Case A: non-owner must be rejected"
    case_b = api_client.patch(
        f"/cards/{card_at_limit['id']}/pin",
        json={"new_pin": <generate_valid_pin>()},
        headers=owner_headers_limited,
    )
    assert case_b.status_code == 429, "Case B: owner at limit must get 429"
    case_c = api_client.patch(
        f"/cards/{fresh_card['id']}/pin",
        json={"new_pin": <generate_valid_pin>()},
        headers=owner_headers_fresh,
    )
    assert case_c.status_code == 200, "Case C: owner under limit must succeed"
